chapter.label: Tale 228
chapter.title: Nissen 9 BHG 1450z¢ be alienated from the joy in which the saints are going to delight.

paragraphs.text: There was going to be holy communion in a community but when 229. NISSEN 10 BHG 1442ch the deacons came int to put on their stoles, one of them could not be found by them. After much searching, they told the abba. He There was a stranger from among the fathers and he told a parable said to them: ‘Search again’. When it was still not found, moved by like this; the irregularity of the matter, the abba said: “We are cohabiting with In my country they dig pits to trap lions. Now it so happened that thieves. As the Lord lives, there will be no communion, nor will we a vixen fell into <one such> pit. Hard thongh it tried to do so, it take a morsel of food until the thief is found.” While the abba, was unable to get out. Then the master of the ditch came by to sce together with the deacons, was searching the brothers’ cells the if anything bad fallen into it—and he found the vixen. The animal brothers <all> sitting in church, the one who stole it said to his dissimulated; it lay there as though it were dead. The master of the neighbour (who was a pious soul): ‘I am in deep trouble! How I am ditch climbed down, grasped it by the tail and threw it up; <the going to suffer!’ The other said: ‘What for? He said: Tam the one vixen> made her escape on the double. Some days later the same who took the stole; it is in my cell, down in a wine-jar for exporting. animal saw another vixen which had fallen into the same ditch; and wine from Gaza>.’ The other said: Do not upset yourself; go and when she saw her, pacing to and fro but unable to get out, she said put it in my cell’, So he went and put the wine-jar in the brother's to her: ‘I tao did a good deal of pacing to and {ro; and, if had not cell. When the abba and the deacons came searching where the made myself dead, I would not have got out’.
